name: budi
description: A quiet status bar item for VS Code and Cursor that shows your AI coding spend over the last 1 day / 7 days / 30 days, scoped to the IDE you're working in. If you don't have budi installed yet, the extension will guide you through the install.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: In untrusted workspaces, budi ignores workspace-scoped overrides for daemonUrl, cloudEndpoint, and pollingIntervalMs. The status bar still works against the user-scoped (or default) daemon.

budi.pollingIntervalMs.description: How often to refresh the status bar (in milliseconds).

budi.daemonUrl.patternErrorMessage: budi.daemonUrl must point at a loopback host (127.0.0.1, localhost, or ::1). Non-loopback values are ignored at runtime.
budi.daemonUrl.description: Base URL for the budi daemon. Must be a loopback host (127.0.0.1, localhost, or ::1) — the daemon is local-only by design.

budi.cloudEndpoint.patternErrorMessage: budi.cloudEndpoint must be an https URL on getbudi.dev (or a subdomain). Off-domain values are ignored at runtime to prevent phishing.
budi.cloudEndpoint.description: Cloud dashboard URL opened when the status bar item is clicked. Must be an https URL on getbudi.dev — off-domain overrides are ignored.

budi.suppressUpdatePrompt.description: When the local daemon's api_version is older than this extension requires, suppress the actionable upgrade toast. The status bar still reads `budi · update needed` so the user is not silently left on a stale daemon. Use this on machines where the daemon is centrally managed and you cannot run `budi update` yourself.
